title: Control System Continuity — Historian Export and OT Backup Health

compliance.assurance_rationale: Uplifted from contributing to partial: UC provides meaningful monitoring evidence for 4.3.4.3.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that IEC 62443 4.3.4.3 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.15.6: Control System Continuity — Historian Export and OT Backup Health.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.15.6' running on index=ot_hist export status; index=backup Commvault/Veeam, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

app: Universal Forwarder on backup/historian bridge hosts
spl:
index=ot_hist sourcetype="hist:export_status" earliest=-72h
| stats latest(_time) as last_ok, latest(status) as st by plant, historian_id
| eval hours_since=round((now()-last_ok)/3600,1)
| where st!="SUCCESS" OR hours_since>26
| append [ search index=backup sourcetype="veeam:JobSession" OR sourcetype="commvault:job" earliest=-72h | where match(JobName,"(?i)OT|Historian|VMware_OT") | stats latest(Result) as st by JobName | where st!="Success" ]
| sort - hours_since
description: Detects missed historian snapshots or failed OT-tier backups that undermine recovery time objectives for control systems.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for IEC 62443 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes backup, ot_hist and sourcetypes commvault:job, hist:export_status, veeam:JobSession. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Emit structured export rows from each historian interface; (2) tag backup jobs covering OT VMs; (3) tune thresholds to RPO; (4) link runbook for cold standby DCS. Indexes required: `index=backup`, `index=ot_hist`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=commvault:job`, `sourcetype=hist:export_status`, `sourcetype=veeam:JobSession`.
visualization: Table (gaps), Single value (failed jobs), Time chart (export lag). Key fields: `JobName`, `historian_id`, `hours_since`, `last_ok`, `plant`, `st`.
knownFalsePositives: Zone communications during authorized engineering, vendor maintenance, or planned commissioning. PAM sessions from authorized engineering access, vendor remote support, or scheduled maintenance.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Universal Forwarder on backup/historian bridge hosts.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=ot_hist` export status; `index=backup` Commvault/Veeam.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Emit structured export rows from each historian interface; (2) tag backup jobs covering OT VMs; (3) tune thresholds to RPO; (4) link runbook for cold standby DCS.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=ot_hist sourcetype="hist:export_status" earliest=-72h
| stats latest(_time) as last_ok, latest(status) as st by plant, historian_id
| eval hours_since=round((now()-last_ok)/3600,1)
| where st!="SUCCESS" OR hours_since>26
| append [ search index=backup sourcetype="veeam:JobSession" OR sourcetype="commvault:job" earliest=-72h | where match(JobName,"(?i)OT|Historian|VMware_OT") | stats latest(Result) as st by JobName | where st!="Success" ]
| sort - hours_since
```

Understanding this SPL

**Control System Continuity — Historian Export and OT Backup Health (IEC 62443-2-1 / 4.3.4.3)** — Detects missed historian snapshots or failed OT-tier backups that undermine recovery time objectives for control systems.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=ot_hist` export status; `index=backup` Commvault/Veeam. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Universal Forwarder on backup/historian bridge hosts. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: ot_hist; **sourcetype**: hist:export_status. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=ot_hist, sourcetype="hist:export_status", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by plant, historian_id** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **hours_since** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where st!="SUCCESS" OR hours_since>26` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Appends rows from a subsearch with `append`.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the OT historian (PI, Proficy) or the vendor HMI/operations console the operator trusts. Spot-check a few source events, reconcile counts, and verify field extractions, thresholds, and index permissions for the people who need this evidence.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (gaps), Single value (failed jobs), Time chart (export lag).
grandmaExplanation: We detects missed historian snapshots or failed OT-tier backups that undermine recovery time objectives for control systems. That helps protect factories, plants, and the gear that runs them from mistakes and bad actors the standards call out.
